## Summary
Build the index builder that scans `~/agents/.tb/` and generates a queryable `index.json`.

## Context
The index is the foundation for all query, enable/disable, and audit operations. It needs to be fast, accurate, and include rich metadata.

## Requirements

### Create src/index-builder.ts
Core module that:
1. Scans `~/agents/.tb/skills/` for skill directories (each containing SKILL.md)
2. Scans `~/agents/.tb/mcp/servers.json` for MCP entries
3. Scans `~/agents/.tb/agents/` for agent profile files
4. Scans `~/agents/.tb/snippets/` for snippet files

### SKILL.md parsing
Extract metadata from SKILL.md:
- Description (from frontmatter `description:` or first paragraph)
- Tags (from frontmatter `tags:` if present)
- Name (from directory name)

### Index schema
Write to `~/agents/.tb/index.json`:

```typescript
interface FacultIndex {
  version: number;
  updatedAt: string;
  skills: { [name: string]: SkillEntry };
  mcp: { servers: { [name: string]: McpEntry } };
  agents: { [name: string]: AgentEntry };
  snippets: { [name: string]: SnippetEntry };
}
```

### Commands
- `facult index` — rebuild index from current state
- `facult index --force` — full reindex, clear any cached state

### CLI integration
Update `src/index.ts` to add the `index` command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Index correctly lists all skills with descriptions
- [ ] Index correctly lists all MCP servers
- [ ] Index includes lastModified timestamps
- [ ] `facult index` completes in < 2s for typical installs
- [ ] Index file is human-readable JSON

## Effort
2 days

## Phase
Phase 3: Normalize & Index

## Dependencies
- T-001 (schema enhancements) should be complete for full provenance

## Summary
Implement query engine for filtering/searching the index, plus `list` and `show` CLI commands.

## Context
Users need to quickly find and inspect skills, MCP servers, and agents. The query engine powers all filtering operations.

## Requirements

### Create src/query.ts
Query engine with:
- Filter by type: `skills`, `mcp`, `agents`, `snippets`
- Filter by enabled-for: `--enabled-for cursor`
- Filter by audit status: `--untrusted`, `--flagged`
- Filter by tags: `--tag automation`
- Full-text search in names and descriptions

### list command
```bash
facult list                    # List all (summary)
facult list skills             # List skills only
facult list mcp                # List MCP servers
facult list agents             # List agent profiles
facult list snippets           # List snippets

# Filters
facult list --enabled-for cursor
facult list --untrusted
facult list --flagged
facult list --json             # JSON output
```

Output format (human):
```
Skills (12)
  github          GitHub CLI integration          cursor, claude
  weather         Weather forecasts               cursor
  ...

MCP Servers (5)
  filesystem      File system access              cursor, claude
  ...
```

### show command
```bash
facult show github             # Show skill details
facult show mcp:filesystem     # Show MCP server (mcp: prefix)
facult show --json github      # JSON output
```

Output includes:
- Full description
- Source/provenance
- Enabled-for tools
- Trust/audit status
- File contents (SKILL.md or server config)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `list` shows all items with key metadata
- [ ] `list skills` filters correctly
- [ ] `--enabled-for` filter works
- [ ] `show` displays full details including content
- [ ] `mcp:` prefix disambiguates MCP servers
- [ ] JSON output available for scripting

## Effort
1–2 days

## Phase
Phase 3: Normalize & Index

## Dependencies
- T-002 (index builder)

## Summary
Implement conservative conflict resolution during consolidation with diff preview and user choice.

## Context
From Council Deliberation: "Auto-merge only when normalized content hashes match. Otherwise, interactive prompt with diff and options (keep-newest, keep-local, keep-remote, keep-both)."

This is critical for safety — we must not silently lose user customizations or merge malicious content.

## Requirements

### Content normalization
Before comparing, normalize content:
- Trim leading/trailing whitespace
- Normalize line endings to LF
- Optionally: normalize JSON formatting for config files

### Hash comparison
```typescript
function contentHash(content: string): string {
  const normalized = normalize(content);
  return Bun.hash(normalized).toString(16);
}
```

### Conflict detection
When consolidating an item that already exists:
1. Compare normalized hashes
2. If identical → auto-merge silently
3. If different → show conflict UI

### Conflict UI (clacks)
```
⚠️  Conflict: skill "github" exists with different content

  Current (consolidated):
    Path: ~/agents/.tb/skills/github/SKILL.md
    Modified: 2026-01-15 10:30
    Size: 2.4 KB

  Incoming (from cursor):
    Path: ~/.cursor/skills/github/SKILL.md
    Modified: 2026-01-28 14:20
    Size: 2.6 KB

  [v] View diff
  [1] Keep current (skip incoming)
  [2] Keep incoming (overwrite)
  [3] Keep both (rename incoming to github-cursor)
  [c] Cancel
```

### Diff preview
When user selects "View diff":
- Show unified diff
- Highlight additions/deletions
- Return to choice prompt after viewing

### --auto modes
For scripting/batch operations:
```bash
facult consolidate --auto keep-newest   # Keep most recently modified
facult consolidate --auto keep-current  # Skip all conflicts
facult consolidate --auto keep-incoming # Overwrite all
```

### Archive originals
When overwriting, archive the replaced version:
- `~/agents/.tb/skills/github/SKILL.md.bak.2026-01-29`
- Or in a separate archive directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identical normalized content auto-merges silently
- [ ] Different content triggers interactive prompt
- [ ] Diff preview shows clear comparison
- [ ] All choice options work correctly
- [ ] `--auto` modes work for batch operations
- [ ] Replaced files are archived, not deleted

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Security Note
From Council: "Avoid auto-merging semantic changes. Merging could hide malicious inserts; require explicit review for differences."

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement "managed mode" where facult takes control of a tool's skill and MCP configuration via backup + symlink pattern.

## Context
From spec: "Per-tool opt-in to let facult manage. Backup existing, symlink to central, facult writes tool-specific configs from central state."

This is the core value prop — one source of truth, many tools consume it.

## Requirements

### manage command
```bash
facult manage cursor
```

Flow:
1. Check if tool is already managed → error if so
2. Detect tool paths (use adapter, see T-007/T-008)
3. Backup existing:
   - `~/.cursor/skills/` → `~/.cursor/skills.bak/`
   - `~/.cursor/mcp.json` → `~/.cursor/mcp.json.bak`
4. Create skills directory if needed
5. Create per-skill symlinks for enabled skills:
   - `~/.cursor/skills/github` → `~/agents/.tb/skills/github`
6. Generate tool-specific `mcp.json` from canonical `servers.json`
7. Record in `~/.facult/managed.json`

### unmanage command
```bash
facult unmanage cursor
```

Flow:
1. Check if tool is managed → error if not
2. Remove symlinks
3. Restore from backup (if exists)
4. Remove from `managed.json`

### managed command
```bash
facult managed
```

Output:
```
Managed tools:
  cursor      Skills: 8 enabled, MCP: 5 servers
  claude      Skills: 12 enabled, MCP: 5 servers
```

### managed.json schema
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "tools": {
    "cursor": {
      "managedAt": "2026-01-29T...",
      "skillsPath": "~/.cursor/skills",
      "mcpPath": "~/.cursor/mcp.json",
      "backups": {
        "skills": "~/.cursor/skills.bak",
        "mcp": "~/.cursor/mcp.json.bak"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Per-skill symlinks
For granular enable/disable:
- Each enabled skill gets its own symlink
- Disabled skills have no symlink
- This allows per-tool skill selection

### Safety checks
- Verify backup succeeded before modifying
- Check for existing symlinks (don't double-manage)
- Validate tool paths exist
- Warn if tool is running (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `manage cursor` backs up and symlinks correctly
- [ ] `unmanage cursor` restores from backup
- [ ] `managed` lists all managed tools
- [ ] Per-skill symlinks work (not directory symlink)
- [ ] Backup restoration is reliable
- [ ] Error handling for permission issues

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Dependencies
- T-007 (adapter architecture) for tool path detection

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement commands to enable/disable skills and MCP servers per-tool, and sync changes to managed tools.

## Context
Once tools are in managed mode, users need fine-grained control over what's enabled where.

## Requirements

### enable command
```bash
facult enable github --for cursor,claude
facult enable mcp:filesystem --for cursor
facult enable weather  # Enable for all managed tools
```

Flow:
1. Validate item exists in index
2. Update `enabledFor` array in index.json
3. For each managed tool in `--for`:
   - Skills: create symlink if not exists
   - MCP: regenerate tool's mcp.json
4. Save updated index

### disable command
```bash
facult disable github --for cursor
facult disable mcp:filesystem --for claude
facult disable weather  # Disable for all
```

Flow:
1. Update `enabledFor` array (remove tools)
2. For each managed tool:
   - Skills: remove symlink
   - MCP: regenerate tool's mcp.json without this server
3. Save updated index

### sync command
```bash
facult sync              # Sync all managed tools
facult sync cursor       # Sync specific tool
facult sync --dry-run    # Show what would change
```

Sync ensures managed tools match current index state:
1. For each managed tool:
   - Add missing symlinks for enabled skills
   - Remove symlinks for disabled skills
   - Regenerate mcp.json from servers.json + enabledFor

### --dry-run output
```
Would sync cursor:
  + Add symlink: github
  - Remove symlink: old-skill
  ~ Regenerate mcp.json (2 servers added, 1 removed)

Would sync claude:
  (no changes)
```

### Batch operations
```bash
facult enable github weather codex --for cursor
```

Enable multiple items at once.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `enable` creates symlinks and updates index
- [ ] `disable` removes symlinks and updates index
- [ ] `sync` brings managed tools into alignment
- [ ] `--dry-run` shows changes without applying
- [ ] MCP config regenerated correctly on enable/disable
- [ ] Batch enable/disable works

## Effort
2 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Dependencies
- T-005 (managed mode)
- T-008 (adapters for MCP generation)

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement strict validation for snippet markers to prevent injection attacks and ensure consistent behavior.

## Context
From Council Security Officer: "Ensure snippet replacement does not allow injection by validating marker names."

Snippet markers use HTML comments: `<!-- fclty:name -->...<!-- /fclty:name -->`

## Requirements

### Valid marker names
Allowed characters:
- Alphanumeric: a-z, A-Z, 0-9
- Hyphens and underscores: -, _
- Forward slashes for scoping: /

Valid examples:
- `fclty:codingstyle`
- `fclty:global/boundaries`
- `fclty:myproject/context`
- `fclty:my-snippet_v2`

### Invalid patterns to reject
- Path traversal: `fclty:../../../etc/passwd`
- Empty names: `fclty:`
- Special characters: `fclty:foo;bar`, `fclty:foo$(cmd)`
- Whitespace: `fclty:foo bar`
- Leading/trailing slashes: `fclty:/foo`, `fclty:foo/`

### Validation function
```typescript
function validateMarkerName(name: string): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
  if (!name) return { valid: false, error: 'Empty marker name' };
  if (name.includes('..')) return { valid: false, error: 'Path traversal not allowed' };
  if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+(\/[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)*$/.test(name)) {
    return { valid: false, error: 'Invalid characters in marker name' };
  }
  return { valid: true };
}
```

### Error messaging
When invalid marker found:
```
Error: Invalid snippet marker "tb:../foo" in CLAUDE.md:15
  Path traversal (..) not allowed in marker names.
  Valid format: fclty:name or fclty:scope/name
  Examples: fclty:codingstyle, fclty:global/boundaries
```

### Integration points
1. Snippet sync command — validate before replacing
2. Snippet show/list — validate when reading
3. Create command — validate name input

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Path traversal rejected
- [ ] Special characters rejected
- [ ] Clear error messages with line numbers
- [ ] Valid scoped names work (tb:global/foo)
- [ ] Regex covers all edge cases

## Effort
1 day

## Phase
Phase 5: Snippets & Templates

## Security Note
This is a security boundary — marker names must not allow arbitrary file access or command injection.
payload.title: T-009: Snippet marker validation

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement the snippet system: storage, lookup, and sync across config files.

## Context
Snippets are reusable blocks that can be shared across CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and other config files. When a snippet is updated, all files using it can be synced.

## Requirements

### Snippet storage
```
~/agents/.tb/snippets/
├── global/
│   ├── codingstyle.md
│   ├── boundaries.md
│   └── tooling.md
└── projects/
    └── myproject/
        └── context.md
```

Each snippet is a plain markdown file containing the content to inject.

### Marker format
```markdown
<!-- fclty:codingstyle -->
## Coding Style

- Use TypeScript strict mode
- Prefer functional patterns
<!-- /fclty:codingstyle -->
```

### Create src/snippets.ts
Core functions:
1. `findSnippet(name: string, project?: string): string | null` — look up snippet content
2. `findMarkers(filePath: string): Marker[]` — find all markers in a file
3. `syncFile(filePath: string, dryRun?: boolean): SyncResult` — sync one file
4. `syncAll(dryRun?: boolean): SyncResult[]` — sync all managed files

### Lookup order (inheritance)
1. `projects/<current>/name.md` (if project context available)
2. `global/name.md`
3. Explicit scope: `global/name` always uses global, `myproject/name` uses project

### Sync algorithm
```typescript
function syncFile(filePath: string): SyncResult {
  const content = await readFile(filePath);
  const markers = findMarkers(content);
  
  let updated = content;
  const changes: Change[] = [];
  
  for (const marker of markers) {
    validate(marker.name);  // T-009
    const snippet = findSnippet(marker.name);
    if (!snippet) {
      changes.push({ marker: marker.name, status: 'not-found' });
      continue;
    }
    if (marker.content !== snippet) {
      updated = replaceMarkerContent(updated, marker, snippet);
      changes.push({ marker: marker.name, status: 'updated' });
    }
  }
  
  if (updated !== content) {
    await writeFile(filePath, updated);
  }
  
  return { filePath, changes };
}
```

### Commands
```bash
facult snippets list                  # List all snippets
facult snippets show codingstyle      # Show snippet content
facult snippets create myboundaries   # Create (opens $EDITOR)
facult snippets edit codingstyle      # Edit existing

facult snippets sync                  # Sync all files
facult snippets sync --dry-run        # Preview changes
facult snippets sync CLAUDE.md        # Sync specific file
```

### Dry-run output
```
Would update CLAUDE.md:
  fclty:codingstyle — updated (15 lines changed)
  fclty:tooling — updated (8 lines changed)

Would update ~/.cursor/AGENTS.md:
  fclty:codingstyle — updated
  fclty:custom — not found (skipped)

2 files would be updated
```

### File discovery for sync
Which files to sync:
1. Explicitly managed agent config files
2. Or scan `~/agents/.tb/agents/` for files with markers
3. User can also specify individual files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snippet lookup with inheritance works
- [ ] Markers correctly identified and replaced
- [ ] Opening/closing markers must match
- [ ] `--dry-run` shows accurate preview
- [ ] Editor integration for create/edit
- [ ] Handles missing snippets gracefully

## Effort
2 days

## Phase
Phase 5: Snippets & Templates

## Dependencies
- T-009 (marker validation)

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement static security analysis for skills and MCP configs using pattern matching.

## Context
From Council: "Static analysis is fast, runs locally. Pattern matching for known-bad (exfil, creds, shell escapes)."

This is the first line of defense — quick checks that can run on every consolidate/install.

## Requirements

### Create src/audit/static.ts
Static analyzer that checks for suspicious patterns.

### Patterns to detect

#### Skills (SKILL.md)
1. **Data exfiltration instructions**
   - "send to external server"
   - "POST data to"
   - "upload contents"
   - "exfiltrate"

2. **Credential access**
   - "read ~/.ssh"
   - "cat /etc/passwd"
   - "access API keys"
   - "read credentials"
   - "dump secrets"

3. **Shell escapes**
   - "execute arbitrary"
   - "run any command"
   - "eval("
   - "$()" in instructions

4. **Suspicious network**
   - IP addresses (outside localhost)
   - Non-HTTPS URLs
   - Unknown domains

5. **Obfuscation**
   - Base64 encoded blocks
   - Hex encoded strings
   - Unusual unicode

#### MCP Servers
1. **Sketchy binaries**
   - Paths outside standard locations
   - `/tmp/` executables
   - Binaries with suspicious names

2. **Dangerous arguments**
   - `--allow-write /`
   - Unrestricted paths
   - `eval` or `exec` flags

3. **Environment exposure**
   - `*_KEY` or `*_SECRET` in plain text
   - Hardcoded tokens

### Rule configuration
```yaml
# ~/.facult/audit-rules.yaml
version: 1
rules:
  - id: exfil-instruction
    severity: high
    pattern: "send.*(to|external|server)"
    message: "Possible data exfiltration instruction"
    
  - id: credential-access
    severity: critical
    pattern: "(read|cat|access).*(ssh|credentials|secrets|api.?key)"
    message: "Possible credential access"
```

### Risk scoring
- **critical**: Immediate security concern, block by default
- **high**: Likely problematic, warn strongly
- **medium**: Suspicious, worth reviewing
- **low**: Minor concern, informational

### Command
```bash
facult audit static                   # Full scan
facult audit static github            # Single skill
facult audit static --severity high   # Filter output
facult audit static --json            # JSON output
```

### Output format
```
Static Security Audit
=====================

❌ github (2 findings)
  [HIGH] credential-access (SKILL.md:45)
    Pattern: "read SSH keys from ~/.ssh"
    → Review this instruction for credential access

  [MEDIUM] network-call (SKILL.md:78)
    Pattern: "fetch from https://api.example.com"
    → Verify this external endpoint is trusted

✅ weather (0 findings)
✅ calendar (0 findings)

Summary: 2 findings in 1 item (1 high, 1 medium)
```

### Result storage
Write to `~/.facult/audit/static-latest.json`:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-29T...",
  "mode": "static",
  "results": [
    {
      "item": "github",
      "type": "skill",
      "findings": [...]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scans SKILL.md content for patterns
- [ ] Scans MCP server configs for suspicious commands
- [ ] Configurable rules via YAML
- [ ] Severity levels work
- [ ] Clear output with locations
- [ ] Results stored for later reference
- [ ] `--json` output for tooling

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 6: Security Audit

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement AI-assisted security audit using coding agents (Codex, Claude, Gemini) for deeper analysis.

## Context
From Council: "Agent reviews each skill/MCP one-by-one. Catches subtle issues static rules miss. Default to full content with optional summary-first mode; record coverage and allow --full override."

Static analysis catches patterns; agents catch intent and subtle issues.

## Requirements

### Create src/audit/agent.ts
Agent-assisted audit system.

### Command
```bash
facult audit --with codex           # Use OpenAI Codex
facult audit --with claude          # Use Claude
facult audit --with gemini          # Use Gemini
facult audit github --with claude   # Single item

# Options
facult audit --with codex --summary      # Summary-first (faster)
facult audit --with claude --full        # Force full content
```

### Prompt design
Default: full content for thorough review.

```markdown
You are a security auditor reviewing agent skills and MCP server configurations.

## Item to Review
Name: github
Type: skill
Path: ~/agents/.tb/skills/github/

## Content
<SKILL.md content here>

## Security Checklist
Review for:
1. Data exfiltration - Does this instruct sending data to external servers?
2. Credential access - Does this access secrets, keys, or sensitive files?
3. Shell injection - Are there unsafe command patterns?
4. Privilege escalation - Does this request elevated permissions?
5. Obfuscation - Is there hidden or encoded content?
6. Scope creep - Does this do more than advertised?

## Response Format
{
  "item": "github",
  "passed": true/false,
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "low|medium|high|critical",
      "category": "category-name",
      "description": "What was found",
      "location": "line or section",
      "recommendation": "What to do"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "Brief overall assessment"
}
```

### Summary mode
For large scans, send abbreviated content:
- First 500 chars of SKILL.md
- Just server name + command for MCP
- Mark audit as "partial" in results

### Coverage tracking
```json
{
  "coverage": "full",        // or "partial"
  "contentChars": 2450,
  "tokensUsed": 1200,
  "model": "claude-3-opus"
}
```

### Agent execution
Options for running agents:
1. Direct API call (requires keys)
2. Shell out to CLI tools (codex, claude, gemini CLIs)
3. Integration with sessions_spawn if running in Clawdbot

For v1, use CLI tools:
```bash
echo "$PROMPT" | claude --print
echo "$PROMPT" | codex -q
echo "$PROMPT" | gemini
```

### Response parsing
Parse JSON response from agent:
- Validate structure
- Handle malformed responses gracefully
- Extract findings and severity

### Output
```
Agent Security Audit (claude-3-opus)
====================================

❌ github
  [HIGH] privilege-escalation
    This skill instructs the agent to run commands as root.
    Location: SKILL.md lines 45-50
    Recommendation: Remove sudo instructions, use least privilege.

✅ weather
  No security concerns found.

Summary: 1 item flagged, 1 passed
Coverage: full (2 items, 4,200 tokens)
```

### Result storage
Write to `~/.facult/audit/agent-latest.json`:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-29T...",
  "mode": "agent",
  "agent": "claude",
  "model": "claude-3-opus",
  "coverage": "full",
  "results": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works with codex, claude, gemini CLIs
- [ ] Full content sent by default
- [ ] Summary mode available with coverage tracking
- [ ] Response parsed correctly
- [ ] Handles agent errors gracefully
- [ ] Results stored with metadata

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 6: Security Audit

## Dependencies
- Agent CLIs must be installed (codex, claude, gemini)

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement trust marking for skills and MCP servers, with filters for untrusted/flagged items.

## Context
From Council: "Per-user trust by default. Trust does not skip audit, only annotates. Optional org trust lists later with signatures/checksums."

Trust is about user confidence, not about bypassing security checks.

## Requirements

### Index fields
Add to each skill and MCP entry:
```typescript
{
  trusted: boolean;           // User has reviewed and trusts
  trustedAt?: string;         // When trust was granted
  trustedBy?: string;         // 'user' or future org identifier
  auditStatus: 'pending' | 'passed' | 'flagged';
  lastAuditAt?: string;
}
```

### trust command
```bash
facult trust github
facult trust mcp:filesystem
```

Flow:
1. Look up item in index
2. Set `trusted: true`, `trustedAt: now`, `trustedBy: 'user'`
3. Save index

Output:
```
✓ Marked github as trusted
  Note: Trust is an annotation. Run `facult audit` for security review.
```

### untrust command
```bash
facult untrust github
```

Flow:
1. Set `trusted: false`, clear trustedAt/trustedBy
2. Save index

### List filters
```bash
facult list --untrusted     # Show items where trusted=false
facult list --flagged       # Show items where auditStatus='flagged'
facult list --pending       # Show items where auditStatus='pending'
```

### Audit integration
After audit completes:
- Update `auditStatus` based on findings
- `passed` if no high/critical findings
- `flagged` if any high/critical findings
- Update `lastAuditAt`

### Trust does NOT skip audit
Important: trust is informational. A trusted item can still be flagged by audit.
```
⚠️  github is trusted but has audit findings:
    [HIGH] credential-access
    Run `facult show github` for details.
```

### Future: Org trust lists (placeholder)
Schema for future org trust lists:
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "org": "hack-dance",
  "trustedItems": [
    { "name": "github", "type": "skill", "hash": "abc123" }
  ],
  "signature": "..."
}
```

For now, just add placeholder in code:
```typescript
// TODO: Support org trust lists (signed JSON)
// See: SPEC.md Phase 6
```

## Context
The canonical root is currently hard-coded as `~/agents/.tb` in multiple places (consolidate/index/manage/query).

Now that the repo/tool name is **facult**, we likely want to support a facult-named root while keeping backward compatibility.

## Proposal
- Support overriding the root directory via either:
  - env var (e.g. `FACULT_ROOT_DIR`), or
  - config file (e.g. `~/.facult/config.json`).
- Backward compatible behavior:
  - If `FACULT_ROOT_DIR` is set: use it.
  - Else if `~/agents/.facult` exists: prefer it.
  - Else fall back to `~/agents/.tb`.

## Acceptance
- All commands that currently assume `~/agents/.tb` work correctly with an overridden root.
- A user can migrate their canonical store without breaking existing installs.

## Context
`facult scan` reads VS Code-like `settings.json` files (Cursor/Windsurf/VS Code). These are often **JSONC** (comments + trailing commas). Using `JSON.parse()` fails and we miss MCP servers declared there.

Example failure (before fix):
- Cursor: `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/settings.json`
  - error: `JSON Parse error: Unrecognized token '/'`

## Fix
- Add a lenient JSON parser that falls back to JSONC.
- Use it in scan JSON reads.

Implementation:
- `src/util/json.ts`: `parseJsonLenient()` (JSON -> JSONC fallback)
- `src/scan.ts`: use `parseJsonLenient()` when reading JSON
- `src/util/json.test.ts`: coverage for comments + trailing commas

## Acceptance
- `facult scan --json` reports **no parse error** for Cursor/VS Code/Windsurf `settings.json` with comments.
- MCP server names under `mcpServers` in these settings files are detected.

## Problem
`facult show mcp:<name>` currently prints the full MCP config file content. MCP configs often contain secrets in `env` (tokens/keys). This makes it easy to leak secrets to logs/scrollback.

Related: index entries store raw MCP `definition` in `~/agents/.tb/index.json` (not in-repo, but still a risk surface).

## Proposal
- Default behavior: redact sensitive values when printing MCP configs.
- Add an explicit escape hatch: `facult show --show-secrets mcp:<name>`.

Redaction strategy (v1):
- For `env`, show keys only or redact values for keys matching: `/(TOKEN|KEY|SECRET|PASSWORD|PASS|BEARER)/i`.
- Consider redacting obvious auth strings in vendorExtensions too.

## Acceptance
- `facult show mcp:<name>` does not print secret values by default.
- `--show-secrets` prints raw values (explicit user intent).
- JSON output is explicit about redaction (e.g. `redacted: true`).

## Problem
`facult show mcp:<name>` printed the full MCP config file content and the indexed MCP definition. MCP configs often contain secrets in `env` (tokens/keys), so printing raw content can leak secrets to scrollback/logs.

## Fix
- Default behavior: redact likely-secret values when showing `mcp:*` entries.
- Escape hatch: `facult show mcp:<name> --show-secrets` prints raw values (explicit intent).

Redaction strategy (v1):
- Redact object values for keys matching: `/(TOKEN|KEY|SECRET|PASSWORD|PASS|BEARER)/i`.
- Also redact obvious token-like strings in free text (e.g. `sk-...`, `ghp_...`).

## Acceptance

- `--show-secrets` prints raw values.
- Added a regression test to prevent accidental reintroduction.

## Summary
Implement AI-assisted security audit using coding agents (Codex, Claude, Gemini) for deeper analysis.

## Context
From Council: "Agent reviews each skill/MCP one-by-one. Catches subtle issues static rules miss. Default to full content with optional summary-first mode; record coverage and allow --full override."

Static analysis catches patterns; agents catch intent and subtle issues.

## Requirements

### Create src/audit/agent.ts
Agent-assisted audit system.

### Command
```bash
facult audit --with codex           # Use OpenAI Codex
facult audit --with claude          # Use Claude
facult audit --with gemini          # Use Gemini
facult audit github --with claude   # Single item

# Options
facult audit --with codex --summary      # Summary-first (faster)
facult audit --with claude --full        # Force full content
```

### Prompt design
Default: full content for thorough review.

```markdown
You are a security auditor reviewing agent skills and MCP server configurations.

## Item to Review
Name: github
Type: skill
Path: <FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/skills/github/

## Content
<SKILL.md content here>

## Security Checklist
Review for:
1. Data exfiltration - Does this instruct sending data to external servers?
2. Credential access - Does this access secrets, keys, or sensitive files?
3. Shell injection - Are there unsafe command patterns?
4. Privilege escalation - Does this request elevated permissions?
5. Obfuscation - Is there hidden or encoded content?
6. Scope creep - Does this do more than advertised?

## Response Format
{
  "item": "github",
  "passed": true/false,
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "low|medium|high|critical",
      "category": "category-name",
      "description": "What was found",
      "location": "line or section",
      "recommendation": "What to do"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "Brief overall assessment"
}
```

### Summary mode
For large scans, send abbreviated content:
- First 500 chars of SKILL.md
- Just server name + command for MCP
- Mark audit as "partial" in results

### Coverage tracking
```json
{
  "coverage": "full",        // or "partial"
  "contentChars": 2450,
  "tokensUsed": 1200,
  "model": "claude-3-opus"
}
```

### Agent execution
Options for running agents:
1. Direct API call (requires keys)
2. Shell out to CLI tools (codex, claude, gemini CLIs)
3. Integration with sessions_spawn if running in Clawdbot

For v1, use CLI tools:
```bash
echo "$PROMPT" | claude --print
echo "$PROMPT" | codex -q
echo "$PROMPT" | gemini
```

### Response parsing
Parse JSON response from agent:
- Validate structure
- Handle malformed responses gracefully
- Extract findings and severity

### Output
```
Agent Security Audit (claude-3-opus)
====================================

❌ github
  [HIGH] privilege-escalation
    This skill instructs the agent to run commands as root.
    Location: SKILL.md lines 45-50
    Recommendation: Remove sudo instructions, use least privilege.

✅ weather
  No security concerns found.

Summary: 1 item flagged, 1 passed
Coverage: full (2 items, 4,200 tokens)
```

### Result storage
Write to `~/.facult/audit/agent-latest.json`:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-29T...",
  "mode": "agent",
  "agent": "claude",
  "model": "claude-3-opus",
  "coverage": "full",
  "results": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works with codex, claude, gemini CLIs
- [ ] Full content sent by default
- [ ] Summary mode available with coverage tracking
- [ ] Response parsed correctly
- [ ] Handles agent errors gracefully
- [ ] Results stored with metadata

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 6: Security Audit

## Dependencies
- Agent CLIs must be installed (codex, claude, gemini)

## Summary
Implement the snippet system: storage, lookup, and sync across config files.

## Context
Snippets are reusable blocks that can be shared across CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and other config files. When a snippet is updated, all files using it can be synced.

## Requirements

### Snippet storage
```
<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/snippets/
├── global/
│   ├── codingstyle.md
│   ├── boundaries.md
│   └── tooling.md
└── projects/
    └── myproject/
        └── context.md
```

Each snippet is a plain markdown file containing the content to inject.

### Marker format
```markdown
<!-- fclty:codingstyle -->
## Coding Style

- Use TypeScript strict mode
- Prefer functional patterns
<!-- /fclty:codingstyle -->
```

### Create src/snippets.ts
Core functions:
1. `findSnippet(name: string, project?: string): string | null` — look up snippet content
2. `findMarkers(filePath: string): Marker[]` — find all markers in a file
3. `syncFile(filePath: string, dryRun?: boolean): SyncResult` — sync one file
4. `syncAll(dryRun?: boolean): SyncResult[]` — sync all managed files

### Lookup order (inheritance)
1. `projects/<current>/name.md` (if project context available)
2. `global/name.md`
3. Explicit scope: `global/name` always uses global, `myproject/name` uses project

### Sync algorithm
```typescript
function syncFile(filePath: string): SyncResult {
  const content = await readFile(filePath);
  const markers = findMarkers(content);
  
  let updated = content;
  const changes: Change[] = [];
  
  for (const marker of markers) {
    validate(marker.name);  // T-009
    const snippet = findSnippet(marker.name);
    if (!snippet) {
      changes.push({ marker: marker.name, status: 'not-found' });
      continue;
    }
    if (marker.content !== snippet) {
      updated = replaceMarkerContent(updated, marker, snippet);
      changes.push({ marker: marker.name, status: 'updated' });
    }
  }
  
  if (updated !== content) {
    await writeFile(filePath, updated);
  }
  
  return { filePath, changes };
}
```

### Commands
```bash
facult snippets list                  # List all snippets
facult snippets show codingstyle      # Show snippet content
facult snippets create myboundaries   # Create (opens $EDITOR)
facult snippets edit codingstyle      # Edit existing

facult snippets sync                  # Sync all files
facult snippets sync --dry-run        # Preview changes
facult snippets sync CLAUDE.md        # Sync specific file
```

### Dry-run output
```
Would update CLAUDE.md:
  fclty:codingstyle — updated (15 lines changed)
  fclty:tooling — updated (8 lines changed)

Would update ~/.cursor/AGENTS.md:
  fclty:codingstyle — updated
  fclty:custom — not found (skipped)

2 files would be updated
```

### File discovery for sync
Which files to sync:
1. Explicitly managed agent config files
2. Or scan `<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/agents/` for files with markers
3. User can also specify individual files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snippet lookup with inheritance works
- [ ] Markers correctly identified and replaced
- [ ] Opening/closing markers must match
- [ ] `--dry-run` shows accurate preview
- [ ] Editor integration for create/edit
- [ ] Handles missing snippets gracefully

## Effort
2 days

## Phase
Phase 5: Snippets & Templates

## Dependencies
- T-009 (marker validation)

## Context
`facult scan --show-duplicates` currently reports duplicates for **skills** only.

For auditing, we also want to detect duplicates across other asset classes:
- MCP servers with the same name defined in multiple config files (Cursor settings, Claude Desktop, canonical store, etc.)
- Hook/rules configs duplicated across sources (e.g. multiple `.claude/settings*.json`, multiple `.cursor/hooks.json`), ideally keyed by kind + normalized content

## Goals
- Add duplicate reporting for MCP servers (keyed by server name)
- Add duplicate reporting for hook/rules assets (keyed by kind + basename, and/or by hash of sanitized content)
- Output stays safe: do not print secret values

## Notes
- MCP duplicates should show: server name, count, and the list of `sourceId:path`.
- Asset duplicates should show: kind, count, and the list of `sourceId:path`.

## Context
Agent-assisted security audit for local coding-assistant assets (skills, MCP servers, hooks) to catch issues that static rules miss.

## Implemented
- Added `src/audit/agent.ts` and wired `facult audit agent ...` via `src/audit/index.ts`.
- Supports `--with claude|codex`, `--from <path>` (repeatable), `--max-items <n>`, and `--json`.
- Runs per-item audits with schema-constrained structured output (Claude uses `--json-schema`; Codex uses `--output-schema`).
- Redacts obvious token formats and secret-ish `KEY=...` assignments before sending content to the agent.
- Writes `~/.facult/audit/agent-latest.json`.

## Notes / Limitations
- Gemini is not implemented.
- Coverage is bounded: skills include `SKILL.md` plus a small, size-limited subset of `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/`.

## Links
- `src/audit/agent.ts`
- `src/audit/index.ts`

## Context
`facult scan --show-duplicates` originally reported duplicates for skills only.

## Implemented
- Extended `facult scan --show-duplicates` to report duplicates across:
  - Skills (same skill name in multiple sources)
  - MCP servers (same server name in multiple config files)
    - Includes a `VAR` column computed from hashing sanitized server definitions (best-effort) so you can spot config drift.
  - Hook/assets (same asset kind + same sanitized content hash)
- Output remains secret-safe (no env values; hashes only).

## Links
- `src/scan.ts`

## Context
`facult scan --from <path>` is useful for scanning many repos, but `--from ~` can be slow/noisy without safety limits.

## Implemented
- Replaced glob-heavy `--from` scanning with a directory walker that:
  - Applies a default ignore list (e.g. `node_modules`, `.git`, `Library`, build outputs)
  - Short-circuits git repos to avoid scanning full working trees (only scans known agent config dirs + `skills/`)
  - Collects skills (`SKILL.md`), MCP configs (`mcp.json`, `mcp.config.json`, etc.), and hook assets (`.claude`, `.cursor`, `.husky`, `.git/hooks`)
  - Emits truncation metadata in scan output (`truncated`, `warnings`)

## New Flags
- `--from-ignore <dir>` (repeatable)
- `--from-no-default-ignore`
- `--from-max-visits <n>`
- `--from-max-results <n>`

## Notes
- Default `--from` visit limits are intentionally conservative; raise limits explicitly when you want exhaustive scans.

## Links
- `src/scan.ts`

## Summary
Build the index builder that scans the canonical store (`FACULT_ROOT_DIR`, default `~/agents/.facult/`) and generates a queryable `index.json`.

## Context
The index is the foundation for all query, enable/disable, and audit operations. It needs to be fast, accurate, and include rich metadata.

## Requirements

### Create src/index-builder.ts
Core module that:
1. Scans `<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/skills/` for skill directories (each containing SKILL.md)
2. Scans `<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/mcp/servers.json` (or `mcp/mcp.json`) for MCP entries
3. Scans `<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/agents/` for agent profile files
4. Scans `<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/snippets/` for snippet files

### SKILL.md parsing

- Description (from frontmatter `description:` or first paragraph)
- Tags (from frontmatter `tags:` if present)

### Index schema

### Commands

- `facult index --force` — full reindex, clear any cached state

### CLI integration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Index correctly lists all skills with descriptions
- [ ] Index correctly lists all MCP servers
- [ ] Index includes lastModified timestamps
- [ ] `facult index` completes in < 2s for typical installs
- [ ] Index file is human-readable JSON

## Effort

## Phase

## Dependencies

## Summary
Implement conservative conflict resolution during consolidation with diff preview and user choice.

## Context
From Council Deliberation: "Auto-merge only when normalized content hashes match. Otherwise, interactive prompt with diff and options (keep-newest, keep-local, keep-remote, keep-both)."

This is critical for safety — we must not silently lose user customizations or merge malicious content.

## Requirements

### Content normalization
Before comparing, normalize content:
- Trim leading/trailing whitespace
- Normalize line endings to LF
- Optionally: normalize JSON formatting for config files

### Hash comparison
```typescript
function contentHash(content: string): string {
  const normalized = normalize(content);
  return Bun.hash(normalized).toString(16);
}
```

### Conflict detection
When consolidating an item that already exists:
1. Compare normalized hashes
2. If identical → auto-merge silently
3. If different → show conflict UI

### Conflict UI (clacks)
```
⚠️  Conflict: skill "github" exists with different content

  Current (consolidated):
    Path: <FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/skills/github/SKILL.md
    Modified: 2026-01-15 10:30
    Size: 2.4 KB

  Incoming (from cursor):
    Path: ~/.cursor/skills/github/SKILL.md
    Modified: 2026-01-28 14:20
    Size: 2.6 KB

  [v] View diff
  [1] Keep current (skip incoming)
  [2] Keep incoming (overwrite)
  [3] Keep both (rename incoming to github-cursor)
  [c] Cancel
```

### Diff preview
When user selects "View diff":
- Show unified diff
- Highlight additions/deletions
- Return to choice prompt after viewing

### --auto modes
For scripting/batch operations:
```bash
facult consolidate --auto keep-newest   # Keep most recently modified
facult consolidate --auto keep-current  # Skip all conflicts
facult consolidate --auto keep-incoming # Overwrite all
```

### Archive originals
When overwriting, archive the replaced version:
- `<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/skills/github/SKILL.md.bak.2026-01-29`
- Or in a separate archive directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identical normalized content auto-merges silently
- [ ] Different content triggers interactive prompt
- [ ] Diff preview shows clear comparison
- [ ] All choice options work correctly
- [ ] `--auto` modes work for batch operations
- [ ] Replaced files are archived, not deleted

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Security Note
From Council: "Avoid auto-merging semantic changes. Merging could hide malicious inserts; require explicit review for differences."

## Summary
Implement "managed mode" where facult takes control of a tool's skill and MCP configuration via backup + symlink pattern.

## Context
From spec: "Per-tool opt-in to let facult manage. Backup existing, symlink to central, facult writes tool-specific configs from central state."

This is the core value prop — one source of truth, many tools consume it.

## Requirements

### manage command
```bash
facult manage cursor
```

Flow:
1. Check if tool is already managed → error if so
2. Detect tool paths (use adapter, see T-007/T-008)
3. Backup existing:
   - `~/.cursor/skills/` → `~/.cursor/skills.bak/`
   - `~/.cursor/mcp.json` → `~/.cursor/mcp.json.bak`
4. Create skills directory if needed
5. Create per-skill symlinks for enabled skills:
   - `~/.cursor/skills/github` → `<FACULT_ROOT_DIR>/skills/github`
6. Generate tool-specific `mcp.json` from canonical `servers.json`
7. Record in `~/.facult/managed.json`

### unmanage command
```bash
facult unmanage cursor
```

Flow:
1. Check if tool is managed → error if not
2. Remove symlinks
3. Restore from backup (if exists)
4. Remove from `managed.json`

### managed command
```bash
facult managed
```

Output:
```
Managed tools:
  cursor      Skills: 8 enabled, MCP: 5 servers
  claude      Skills: 12 enabled, MCP: 5 servers
```

### managed.json schema
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "tools": {
    "cursor": {
      "managedAt": "2026-01-29T...",
      "skillsPath": "~/.cursor/skills",
      "mcpPath": "~/.cursor/mcp.json",
      "backups": {
        "skills": "~/.cursor/skills.bak",
        "mcp": "~/.cursor/mcp.json.bak"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Per-skill symlinks
For granular enable/disable:
- Each enabled skill gets its own symlink
- Disabled skills have no symlink
- This allows per-tool skill selection

### Safety checks
- Verify backup succeeded before modifying
- Check for existing symlinks (don't double-manage)
- Validate tool paths exist
- Warn if tool is running (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `manage cursor` backs up and symlinks correctly
- [ ] `unmanage cursor` restores from backup
- [ ] `managed` lists all managed tools
- [ ] Per-skill symlinks work (not directory symlink)
- [ ] Backup restoration is reliable
- [ ] Error handling for permission issues

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Dependencies
- T-007 (adapter architecture) for tool path detection

## Context
After "consolidate", the canonical MCP registry is written to "~/agents/.facult/mcp/mcp.json". The index builder only looked at "servers.json", so "list mcp" could be empty.

## Fix

## Acceptance

## Summary

- New commands:

  - `facult untrust <name> [moreNames...]`

- Audit integration:
  - `facult audit static` and `facult audit agent` now best-effort update `index.json` audit fields for canonical items.
- Index rebuild safety:
  - `facult index` now preserves enabledFor/trust/audit metadata when rebuilding an existing index.

## Summary
Implement snippet storage + lookup + sync across config files, with a `facult snippets ...` CLI.

## Implemented

  - Marker parsing + pairing: `findMarkersInText()` (with validation and error reporting)
  - Snippet lookup with inheritance: `findSnippet({ marker, project, rootDir? })`
    - `fclty:name` prefers `snippets/projects/<project>/name.md` (if git project detected), then `snippets/global/name.md`
    - `fclty:global/name` forces `snippets/global/name.md`

## Context
Users often run `facult scan --from ~` to inventory project-level hooks and skills across many repos. The previous default `--from-max-visits` (50k) caused frequent truncation on typical home directories, producing surprisingly few results.

## Fix
- Increased default `--from` safety limits:
  - `maxVisits`: 200,000 (was 50,000)
  - `maxResults`: 20,000 (was 5,000)
- Reduced unnecessary traversal while walking `--from` roots:
  - Skip unknown hidden directories (only scan known dot-dirs like `.cursor`, `.claude`, `.husky`, etc.)
  - Added common non-code home directories to the default ignore list (Desktop, Documents, Downloads, etc.)

## Acceptance
- `facult scan --from ~` should usually complete without truncation on a typical dev machine.
- Users can still override limits via `--from-max-visits` / `--from-max-results`.

## Links
- `src/scan.ts`

## Context

## Implemented
- Scan:

- Static audit:

- Agent audit:

## Context
We now inventory and audit a lot of *script-like* and *instruction-like* assets across projects (git hooks, husky hooks, CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, Cursor rules, Claude settings hooks).

Static audit currently focuses on a small set of high-signal patterns and MCP checks. It should get a few additional default rules that are tuned for hook scripts and “download+execute” style risks.

## Proposed Work
- Add a small set of high-signal default static rules that apply to assets/instructions too (while avoiding huge false-positive rates), for example:
  - `curl|wget` piped to `bash|sh` (critical)
  - `base64 -d` piped to `bash|sh` (high)
  - `eval` in hook scripts (high)
  - `chmod +x` in `/tmp` (high)
  - non-HTTPS URLs in scripts/instructions (medium)

- Consider differentiating rule targets (skill vs asset) so we can tune patterns per-asset-kind (optional).

## Acceptance
- Running `facult audit static --from ~` highlights clearly dangerous “download and execute” patterns in hook assets.
- Default rules remain reasonably low-noise for normal dev hooks.

payload.title: CLI: make interactive audit the default; gate legacy under --non-interactive

payload.body:
## Context
facult aims to inventory/audit agent assets "universally". Default scan sources covered macOS and some Linux-style paths, but Windows locations were missing.

## Implemented
- Added Windows default candidates for common tools (under `~/AppData/Roaming/...`):
  - Cursor: `Cursor/User/settings.json` and `Cursor/mcp.json`
  - VS Code / VSCodium: `Code/User/settings.json` and `VSCodium/User/settings.json`
  - Windsurf: `Windsurf/User/settings.json`
  - Claude Desktop: `Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
  - Codex: `openai/codex.json` and `OpenAI/codex.json`
- Added `AppData` to the default `--from` ignore list so `facult scan --from ~` stays usable on Windows.
- Added regression tests for Windows-style settings.json discovery: `src/scan.test.ts`.

## Acceptance
- `bun test` passes.
- On Windows, default `facult scan` finds the same classes of assets (skills + MCP configs + instruction files) without manual `--from`.
- Windows path discovery is covered by unit tests (runs on non-Windows CI using fixtures under a temp home dir).

## Context
The interactive audit wizard should make agent-assisted audits usable at scale:
- show progress while the agent is running
- allow auditing all items (not just an arbitrary max)
- when agent audit runs, review should default to agent-scored results (and show agent notes)
- review should include warnings, not just hard failures
- combined view should not drop agent results when the same item exists in static + agent reports

## Implemented
- `facult audit` wizard improvements:
  - `Max items to send to the agent` accepts `all` / `0` (internally treated as unlimited).
  - Streaming progress updates during agent audit via spinner message: `Agent audit (i/total) type:item`.
  - When both static + agent reports exist, wizard prompts which results to review and defaults to **agent**.
  - Review list includes warnings (any item with findings), not only failures.
  - Item details show agent-provided `notes` when present.
  - Combined view merges static+agent findings instead of dropping agent results.
- Non-interactive audit output now labels `WARN` vs `OK`/`FAIL` for items with findings.
- Updated docs/help to reflect `--non-interactive` and `--max-items all`.

## Acceptance
- `bun test` passes.
- Wizard defaults to reviewing agent findings when agent audit runs.
- `--max-items all` works in both CLI and wizard.
payload.title: Audit UX: agent progress, warnings, and review selection

payload.body:
## Context
`facult audit --with codex` (agent-assisted audit) was failing for every item with `agent-error` findings.

Root cause: Codex's `--output-schema` path uses a strict schema validator that requires `required` arrays to include every key listed in `properties` (no optional properties). Our schema had optional `recommendation/location/notes`.

## Fix
- Updated `src/audit/agent.ts` JSON schema so that every object has `required` including **all** properties.
- Updated the prompt to instruct the agent to use empty strings for non-applicable fields.
- Normalized empty-string `notes/location/recommendation` to `undefined` in stored findings for cleaner UX.

## Validation
- Confirmed a real `facult audit --non-interactive --with codex --max-items 1` run returns structured findings (no schema error).
- `bun test` passes.

## Files
- `src/audit/agent.ts`
